description: An Americana singer-songwriter.
Catch the unique sounds of Smoking Toad, a Santa Fe-based Americana and country band led by Liam Downey, as they bring an electrifying blend of rock, blues, and R&B to select nights.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.

artist: eclectic electric music by outer monkeys
description: Announcing outer monkeys, an eclectic collab between singer Hannah Loomis and guitarist Ross Hamlin! Join us for our first show, Sunday June 14, 6-8pm @ Nuckolls Brewing in the Railyard. Free admission. Covering Lenny Kravitz, Prince, Pretenders, Amy Winehouse + more, plus originals with pop, jazz, R&B, + rock influences
Songwriter Sara Douga will be performing her country tunes at La Reina for an intimate night of Americana and margaritas.
An afternoon of spicy, deep-flavored rock.
Live traditional and contemporary bluegrass music.
A three-piece indie-folk band from Fort Collins, Colorado.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.

A singer-songwriter with an award or two under his belt.
Enjoy Americana tunes with Johnny Lloyd & Co. on the patio at Tumbleroot Brewery.
Austin-based Bluegrass / alt country act Gus Miller and Americana/roots artist Rachel Cole will be playing La Reina. Looks like another fantastic time at everyones favorite Cowboy dress-up saloon.
Nashville-infused folk/Americana.
Thurman Love has a new EP FolkHub, and he's pretty psyched to share the tunes on the Tumbleroot patio.
An unforgettable evening of live country music, good vibes, and great company.

artist: Remix Wednesdays feat. DJ Stranger Vinyl Set
description: Enjoy "Night Snacks with a Soundtrack" at Remix Audio Bar with DJ Stranger curating an all vinyl hip hop set. Try our in house made ice cream for dessert.
Swing those mid-week blues away with the help of the Santa Fe Great Big Jazz Band.
More Americana for the patrons of La Reina!  Grab your hat, your boots and ready yourself for a couple classy cocktails.
Reggae, dancehall, afrobeat and reggaeton.
The Lowtimers are a west-coast Americana band rooted in the road-worn songwriting of Austin Smith and Mark Tegio. Tommy Luke’s songs carry the dusty, well-worn sounds of western music.
Share happy hour with Santa Fe's own musical legend Bill Hearne.
Enjoy some delicious scratch made sourdough bread, tea, friends, and music on vinyl. This session's featured vinyl is "Promises" by Floating Points, Pharoah Sanders and the London Symphony Orchestra.
Maus plays blues, soul and pop on piano and vocals.
A New England-based Americana and blues singer-songwriter.

Texan Americana songwriter Vincent Neil Emerson is playing at the Santa Fe Railyard as part of Lensic 360's summer o' fun!  Opening the outdoor bash is JD Nash & The Rash of Cash.  It's gonna be a smash well worth crashing!
DJ Optamystik taking requests and mixing all your favorites from oldies to today's hits mixing up all genres.
Tony-award winning pianist /vocalist performs an evening of Broadway, pop, and originals in a gorgeous setting!
The most positive beats in town every Thursday.
An Afro-Indigenous country-blues artist whose 'Rebel Folk' sound blends Southwestern grit with Southern roots. His music channels the spirit and stories of life in New Mexico.
A fifth-generation Texan whose roots run deep in the state, giving life to his his Americana/folk repertoire.
The project of singer-songwriter Alex Duckart, Searows is bringing moody tunes to Meow Wolf for a night of folklore-inspired indie.
Original music from a Madrid alt-rock duo.
You heard 'em...your favorite standards and jazz arrangements.

An unforgettable evening filled with live music, delectable food and refreshing drinks in the iconic Santa Fe Railyard. Don't miss this opportunity to be a part of the thriving music community in Santa Fe.

description: The Artisan String Quartet perform compositions that are TBA.

If you've seen any of Sir Woman's shows in the past, you probably already know that the Texas funk outfit is as charismatic as they are musically competent.  These guys are a hell of a ride live if you got your dancin' boots on.  ABQ's own Sweet Roll will be opening up the show.
Rock/folk/Americana.

One Last Funky Dollar is a speciality show presented by One More Silver Dollar featuring the Burque Horns. Its a funky soul dance party featuring the music of Aretha, Otis, Pickett, Dr John and many more funky favorites.
If their New Orleans funk is as good as the New Orleans fried chicken, then we're all in for a treat.

venue: New Mexico School for the Arts
description: Music of Bach, Leclair, and Handel featuring Jennifer Perez, soprano; Stephen Redfield, violin and Kathleen McIntosh, harpsichord. Admission is by donation; suggested donation $20. No reservations needed.
An Americana and bluegrass duo.
Strings shredder Sudan Archives will be lighting up the Railyard as part of a celebration of Juneteenth!  Note that the show is on the 20th not the actual holiday.
Are The Jakes more or less than Less Than Jake? You be the judge.

artist: AJW Presents iNK oN pAPER & Kodama Trio
description: Avant Jazz Workshop presents a birthday celebration of Milton Villarrubia III with two of his favorite groups iNK oN pAPER and The Kodama Trio.
An evening of swinging favorites. Featuring original musical arrangements by writer and bandleader Robert Beasley, this 14-piece jazz orchestra will keep your toe tapping all evening, or dancing.

Lensic 360's Summer Scene rambles on with Frontera Bugalú, Radio Malilla and DJ Luz Skylarker—and this cumbia-focused evening is free and in a nice park.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.
Live traditional and contemporary bluegrass music.
Americana, bluegrass and folk-rock join forces (as they tend to do) when these West Coasters take over the Plaza for a free Summer Scene Show. Squash Blossom Boys open.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.
Darin Savage plays Spanish guitar every Monday evening.

The Samoan/Australian singer-songwriter comes to town by way of uke-ular power, which is to say he's all about the ukulele when he's droppin' tropical folk, reggae and Polynesian jamz. Part of the Santa Fe Summer Scene.
The Pennsylvania indie-pysch champs (and synth aficionados) come to everybody's favorite Midtown venue. Heads up? This will likely sell out as we get closer to the show.
Rock and freaking roll, man. The kind with big-ass guitars and all the emotions and super-sweet drums and guitars and bigness that such a genre entails.
Surfy guitar riffage by way of Link Wray and The Ventures, but also luchadore masks. Part of the Santa Fe Summer Scene series from Lensic 360—and free!
Jazzy jazz and bluesy blues come together for some of that good ol' jump, jive and wailin'. Jam band fands? Take note.
The most positive beats in town every Thursday.
David Wood plays the American Songbook on grand piano every Friday evening.

Local multi-hyphenate Raashan Ahmad presides over his annual ode to hip-hop's genesis and enduring excellence with performances from Souls of Mischief, Brother Ali and Slum Village. Honestly? This lineup is INSANE and the show is free. Thanks, Raashan and Lensic 360!
This-here Denver band blends jazz, electro swing and pysch-rock into this almost bluegrassy-but-also-its-own-thing kind of thing. Part of the Summer Scene series.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.
Live traditional and contemporary bluegrass music.

Pop and jazz merges with Latin American styles like cumbia, salsa and more for another free Plaza show from the Colombian duo of Monsieur Periné and the Lensic 360 folks. Santa Fe's Christina Gomez y los Cuates opens.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.
Darin Savage plays Spanish guitar every Monday evening.
He might be Canadian-born, but Farmer has lived in Santa Fe so long that we're claiming him as a hometown hero. Find Farmer and friends digging into the blues on the Plaza.
Rock and pop by way of Tom Petty and the Stones, sure, but also weirdos like Yo La Tengo and Cat Power.
The most positive beats in town every Thursday.
Keith Secola, Levi Platero and Sage Bond come together to celebrate Indigenous art and music at this free and family-friendly Railyard event. Think blues and rock and stuff.

The free Santa Fe Summer Scene series keeps scene-ing and summer-ing with performances from hip-hop/rock/reggae/funk act Tropidelic and Santa Fe's Boomroots.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.
Darin Savage plays Spanish guitar every Monday evening.

If we're talkin' hometown heroes, the convo should surely include David and Michael Manzanares, whose eponymous band so seamlessly brings together rock and Latin sounds that we've all been pretty into it for, like, forever. Simpatico opens.
If you like the blues (by which we mean the music, not feeling sad), look no further than songwriter/singer/shredder Harrell. Turquoise Steel opens.
The most positive beats in town every Thursday.

The show with Callahan (aka/fka Smog) proved so popular in the ticket sale window that Lensic 360 brass had to move it to a bigger venue. For the record, that venue is The Bridge and the style is DIY and lo-fi indie rock. It's honestly so so good what this dude does. Producer/performer Frances Chang opens the show.

The Oakland singer-songwriter returns for what is starting to feel almost like an annual Santa Fe appearance. This one's for fans of soul, R&B and roots and blues and Grammy winners who are also apparently super-nice dudes.
David Wood plays the American Songbook on grand piano every Friday evening.
Austinite Morales comes to the farm with her mix of Mexican- and American-inspired ranchera, rock, folk and Americana. Part of the free Summer Scene series.

If you don't know who this band is by now, the show is probably not for you. As for everyone else living with Warren Haynes reverence? Your ship has come in. Please note there are multiple ticket tiers, parking options and more vie the Lensic 360 website (click that "website" button above).
Producer/performer Yung Bae pulls from his extensive catalog of dance jamz that feature the likes of funk, hip-hop and disco—plus influences like video games. anime and being a little weird.
Standards, Broadway, originals and much more performed on piano and vocals.

venue: The Bridge at Santa Fe Brewing Co.
description: Pepper and The Movement are two different bands, btw, and not, as we thought, the name of one band. Cool? Cool. The former blends reggae and rock with punk-ish leanings, while the latter is all about alt.reggae. Word.
